# Who Do I Work For? — Employer Intelligence Platform

> Know who you're really working for before you sign.

## What is this?

Who Do I Work For? is an Employer Intelligence platform created by Jackye Clayton. It connects policy, company behavior, workforce signals, compensation patterns, hiring technology use, and career strategy into a single intelligence layer.

## Who is it for?

- **Candidates**: Research any employer before accepting an offer. See political influence, compensation benchmarks, workforce stability, and hiring technology signals.
- **Employees**: Understand what kind of company you're inside. Track influence exposure, culture signals, and leadership behavior.
- **Recruiters & HR professionals**: Audit your Employee Value Proposition against real data. Anticipate candidate objections.
- **Sales & Enterprise teams**: Understand buying committees, workforce priorities, and the political context that shapes procurement.
- **Journalists & Researchers**: Source-linked corporate intelligence with every claim cited to public filings.

## Core Tools

### The Receipts
Company-by-company investigations connecting corporate values claims to political spending, DEI actions, and labor impact. Every claim sourced. Every dollar traced.

### Company Intelligence
Full employer intelligence report for any company. Includes workforce signals, hiring technology signals, compensation patterns, culture signals, influence mapping, and an Employer Clarity Score.

### Compare Companies
Side-by-side comparison of two employers across Employer Clarity Scores, lobbying, PAC spending, workforce signals, and transparency metrics.

### Follow the Money
Trace PAC donations, lobbying expenditures, and political spending from any company to the candidates and bills they support.

### Career Map
Explore career paths with intelligence-backed insights. Understand role progressions, skill gaps, and industry trajectories powered by public data.

### Recruiter Brief
Generate signal-grounded intelligence briefs for candidate conversations. Honest, non-salesy framing backed by real data.

### Signal Alerts
Monitor companies for new signals — WARN Act filings, lobbying changes, OSHA complaints, executive turnover — delivered in real time.

### Workplace DNA Quiz
Discover what matters to you in a workplace — so the platform can match you with companies that actually live it.

### Submit a Tip
Report employer misconduct anonymously. Reports are verified against public records and published if they check out.

### Job Board
Browse job listings enriched with employer transparency signals, Employer Clarity Scores, and values alignment data.

## Data Sources

- FEC campaign finance filings
- Senate lobbying disclosures
- USAspending.gov federal contracts
- BLS wage and employment data
- SEC EDGAR corporate filings
- FRED economic indicators
- OpenSecrets
- Google Patents
- BEA industry data

Every signal links to its source. No opinions. No ratings. Just evidence.

## About Jackye Clayton

Jackye Clayton is a career strategist, HR intelligence expert, and candidate advocate with over 20 years of experience in human resources, recruiting, and talent strategy. She created Who Do I Work For? because candidates deserve full information before making career decisions.

## Contact

- Website: https://wdiwf.jackyeclayton.com
- Advisory services: https://wdiwf.jackyeclayton.com/work-with-jackye

## Pricing

- I'm Curious (Free): Receipts, basic intelligence, Workplace DNA quiz
- The Signal ($49/month): Full dossiers, career intelligence, signal alerts
- The Match ($149/month): Recruiter tools, values-based matching, recruiter briefs
- The Closer ($199 one-time): Deep-dive dossier for a single company
